title: ranunculus acris subsp. pumilus
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. pumilus, also known as the Alpine Buttercup, is a flowering plant belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. It is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair. Herbal preparations of the Alpine Buttercup include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
